Title: Phytochromes mediate germination inhibition under red, far-red, and white light in Aethionema arabicum

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Heme oxygenase family, HO group I
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively cleaves heme to biliverdin IXα, supplying precursor for phytochrome chromophore biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: phytochrome chromophore / active photoreversible phytochrome pool
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phytochrome chromophore formation and thereby enables red/far-red-light inhibition of seed germination; promoter deletion reducing AearHO1 expression abolishes red/far-red inhibition and causes white-light hyposensitive germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in seedlings and seeds; reduced to ~15% in koy-1 seedlings and ~10%–13% in koy-1 seeds relative to WT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in dark-grown seedlings and imbibed seeds; expression largely light independent but strongly reduced in koy-1 due to promoter deletion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Continuous white, red, far-red, and blue light during seed germination; dark control; light intensity strongly affects phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Forward genetic screen; promoter deletion confirmation by PCR; backcross co-segregation; RT-qPCR; in vivo phytochrome spectroscopy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. koy-1 carries a 324 bp promoter deletion in AearHO1 that co-segregates with light-insensitive germination and long hypocotyl phenotype under far-red light. 2. The deletion reduces AearHO1 transcript abundance to about 10%–15% of WT and nearly eliminates photoreversible phytochrome. 3. Loss of AearHO1 function specifically abolishes red- and far-red-mediated inhibition of germination while leaving blue-light inhibition intact, revealing phytochrome-dependent and independent pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Etiolated seedlings (photoreversible phytochrome measured in vivo)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shade-avoidance-like morphology, long hypocotyls/internodes, pale green color, reduced chlorophyll and anthocyanin responses under red/far-red light, altered fruit morph ratio
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames AearHO1 as a key enzyme for chromophore biosynthesis and the causative gene in koy-1; residual expression appears sufficient for very-low-fluence germination induction but insufficient for high-irradiance inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate light sensitivity of germination and propagation traits, but pleiotropic photomorphogenic and reproductive effects make it unsuitable for simple crop engineering without caution
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for phytochrome chromophore supply
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter; koy-1 carries promoter deletion
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Illumina pooled resequencing for mapping-by-sequencing; transcriptome analysis referenced for transcription start site identification; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enables phytochrome light perception that regulates seed germination and other photomorphogenic responses
